title: Why PHP is Still Relevant in 2024
excerpt: Despite newer technologies emerging every year, PHP continues to power over 77% of all websites. Discover why PHP remains a top choice for web development.
content: PHP has been powering the web for nearly three decades, and in 2024, it remains one of the most widely used server-side languages. With PHP 8.x, the language has seen significant performance improvements and modern features like named arguments, union types, fibers, and enums. WordPress alone powers over 43% of all websites on the internet. The Laravel framework has transformed PHP development with its elegant syntax and built-in features for authentication, routing, queuing, and caching. PHP developers remain in high demand, especially for Laravel and WordPress projects.
author: Rajesh Sharma

title: Top 10 Tips for Getting Your First IT Job
excerpt: Breaking into the IT industry can be challenging. Here are 10 proven strategies to help fresh graduates land their first tech job.
content: Landing your first IT job is one of the most exciting milestones in a tech career. Build a strong portfolio with 3-5 projects hosted on GitHub. Master fundamentals in data structures and algorithms. Get certified from AWS, Google, or Microsoft. Network actively on LinkedIn. Tailor your resume for each application. Practice mock interviews on platforms like Pramp or InterviewBit. Learn Git and version control - it is non-negotiable. Contribute to open source projects. Apply broadly to startups, mid-size companies, and service firms. Stay consistent through the 2-6 month job search journey.
author: Priya Gupta

title: WordPress vs Laravel: Which to Choose?
excerpt: Two of the most popular PHP-based platforms — when should you choose WordPress and when should you go with Laravel? A comprehensive comparison.
content: Choosing between WordPress and Laravel depends heavily on your requirements. WordPress is a CMS designed for non-technical users — it excels at content sites, e-commerce with WooCommerce, and quick deployment. Laravel is a PHP framework for building custom web applications with complex business logic, APIs, and scalability requirements. Use WordPress for content-driven websites and Laravel for custom web applications.
author: Amit Pandey

title: Digital Marketing Strategies for Small Businesses
excerpt: Discover effective and budget-friendly digital marketing strategies that small businesses can implement to grow their online presence.
content: In today's digital age, having an online presence is essential for small businesses. Claim and optimize your Google My Business listing for local searches. Focus on 2-3 social media platforms where your audience is active. Create a content marketing blog with keyword-rich articles. Build an email list from day one. Optimize for local SEO with location-based keywords. Start Google Ads with a small budget. Leverage short-form video content on YouTube Shorts and Instagram Reels.
author: Sneha Verma

title: Getting Started with Android Development in 2024
excerpt: A complete guide for beginners to start their Android development journey in 2024, covering languages, tools, and first steps.
content: Android is the world's most popular mobile operating system, powering over 3 billion devices. For 2024, choose Kotlin — it is the officially recommended language. Set up Android Studio, install the Android SDK, and create a virtual device for testing. Learn Activities, Fragments, and the Activity Lifecycle. Use Jetpack Compose for modern declarative UI. Integrate Firebase for authentication, Firestore, and push notifications. Build personal projects to showcase your skills.
author: Vikram Singh

title: Full Stack Development: The Complete Roadmap
excerpt: A comprehensive roadmap for becoming a full-stack developer in 2024, covering frontend, backend, databases, DevOps, and beyond.
content: Full stack development is one of the most in-demand skill sets in tech. Start with frontend: HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript ES6+, then React with Hooks and React Router. For backend, learn Node.js and Express for RESTful API design. Study databases - both SQL (MySQL/PostgreSQL) and NoSQL (MongoDB). Master Git, npm, Postman. Learn deployment with Vercel/Netlify for frontend and Railway/Render for backend. The MERN stack is the most popular choice in 2024. Build 5 portfolio projects to showcase your skills.
author: Arjun Kumar
